perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means any information disclosed (whether actual or potential, whether before or after the date of this Agreement, whether in writing, in electronic format, verbally or by any other means and whether directly or indirectly) by or on behalf of the Client to the Company, which relates to the Services including but not exclusively information about venues, prints, contractors, customers, data, employees, sponsors, suppliers, policies, prices, discounts, business development, financial plans, marketing development, manpower plans, methods, services, ideas, projects, rights, products, project development and all other information which might cause considerable harm to the Client and/or to the Client’s business were it to be available and/or used by any third party;
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means any information disclosed (whether actual or potential, whether before or after the date of this Agreement, whether in writing, in electronic format, verbally or by any other means and whether directly or indirectly) by or on behalf of the Client to the Company, which relates to the Services including but not exclusively information about venues, prints, contractors, customers, data, employees, sponsors, suppliers, policies, prices, discounts, business development, financial plans, marketing development, manpower plans, methods, services, ideas, projects, rights, products, project development.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and all other information which might cause considerable harm to the Client and/or to the Client’s business were it to be available and/or used by any third party;' from the definition of Confidential Information, it creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement relies on this broad definition to protect sensitive information. The change narrows the scope of what's considered confidential, potentially leaving critical data unprotected, which contradicts the initial intention of protecting client interests as stated in the background section. Other sections referencing 'Confidential Information' now operate under a less comprehensive definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure Agreement Background MariAl Associates Ltd are conscious of the sensitive nature of texts supplied to us for translation. We have confidentiality agreements in place with our translators and would be happy to provide a blanket agreement or sign a specific agreement before commencing any work. Please note that any confidential information is destroyed once the matter has completed unless reasonably required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause 'and all other information which might cause considerable harm to the Client and/or to the Client’s business were it to be available and/or used by any third party;' from the definition of Confidential Information. This narrows the definition of confidential information. The background section states that MariAl Associates Ltd are conscious of the sensitive nature of texts supplied for translation and have confidentiality agreements in place. By narrowing the definition of confidential information, the changed text now contradicts the background section's intention to protect sensitive client information, as some information that was previously considered confidential under the original definition might no longer be protected under the changed definition. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the agreement relies on a broad definition to protect sensitive information, which is now undermined, aligning with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party, save as provided in Clause 3 hereunder or as required by any applicable laws, without the prior written consent of the Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'save as provided in Clause 3 hereunder or as required by any applicable laws, without the prior written consent of the Client' from Section 1, the clause now directly contradicts Clause 3, which allows for subcontracting and information disclosure to subcontractors. The original clause permitted disclosure under specific conditions (Clause 3 or legal requirements), but the removal creates an absolute restriction on disclosure, conflicting with the allowances made in Clause 3. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the permissible disclosure of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.The Company hereby discloses that it may subcontract all or part of the Services provided to the Client. In such event the Company will use its reasonable endeavours to ensure that all of its Subcontractors, to whom disclosure of the Information is made, act in accordance with the terms of this Agreement as if each were a party hereto. Any third party to whom Confidential Information is provided shall be informed of the confidential nature of the information disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 1 allowed for disclosure of confidential information under Clause 3 or as required by law. The changed text removes this exception, creating an absolute prohibition on disclosure. This directly contradicts Clause 3, which explicitly permits subcontracting and disclosure of information to subcontractors, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof. The Company shall only retain copies of any Confidential Information if necessary in completing the provision of the Services. Any Confidential Information shall at all times be stored securely and the Company shall take reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure. Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company undertakes to return all such documentation at the request of the Client and, in any event, upon conclusion of the Company's need thereof. The Company shall only retain copies of any Confidential Information if necessary in completing the provision of the Services. Any Confidential Information shall at all times be stored securely.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and the Company shall take reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure. Employee’s and agents of the Company shall only be given access to the Confidential Information in order to proceed with the Services.' it introduces an in-text contradiction within section 1. While the company is still obligated to store data securely, the explicit requirement to take 'reasonable precautions to prevent unauthorized disclosure' and restrict access to employees and agents is removed. This implicitly contradicts the section's overarching goal of protecting confidential information, because it no longer mandates actions to prevent unauthorized disclosures. This omission contradicts the principle that the company should protect the client's interest by maintaining data safe and away from unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.The Company acknowledges that all Confidential Information made available or disclosed to it by the Client shall remain the exclusive property of the Client and recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated 'reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorised disclosure' and restricted access to employees and agents, directly supporting the client's confidential information. The changed text removes this explicit requirement. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Company 'recognises that such documentation is confidential and proprietary to the Client and shall be treated as such and shall not be disclosed to a third party.' By removing the obligation to take reasonable precautions, the changed text weakens the protection of confidential information, creating a contradiction regarding the company's responsibility to maintain confidentiality as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 2.The Company undertakes to treat all Confidential Information not in the public domain that it may become a party to as a result of providing the Services to the Client and its associates, including the existence of the request for the Services, the names of the parties involved and any information contained in or which otherwise reflects the information in the documentation received from the Client, as confidential. The Company further undertakes that such Information shall not be disclosed to a third party, save as provided in Clause 4 below, without the prior written consent of the Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.The Company undertakes to treat all Confidential Information not in the public domain that it may become a party to as a result of providing the Services to the Client and its associates, including the existence of the request for the Services, the names of the parties involved and any information contained in or which otherwise reflects the information in the documentation received from the Client, as confidential. The Company further undertakes that such Information shall not be disclosed to a third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'save as provided in Clause 4 below, without the prior written consent of the Client.' from section 2, it creates an in-text contradiction with section 4. Section 4 outlines instances where information isn't considered confidential (publicly available, known to the public, authorized disclosure, etc.). By omitting the reference to Clause 4, Section 2 now imposes an absolute restriction on disclosure, conflicting with the exceptions provided in Clause 4. It introduces uncertainty about when the company can legitimately disclose information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.The parties agree that information is not to be regarded as Confidential Information and that the Company will have no obligation with respect to any information which the Company can demonstrate: 4.1is publicly available at the time of disclosure; 4.2is or becomes known to the public through no wrongful act of the Company; 4.3is used or disclosed with the prior written authorisation of the Client; or 4.4is disclosed by the Company in compliance with a legal requirement of a government agency or otherwise where disclosure is required by operation of law.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 allowed disclosure of Confidential Information as per Clause 4. The changed text in Section 2 removes this allowance, creating an absolute restriction on disclosure. This directly contradicts Section 4, which outlines specific instances where information isn't considered confidential and can be disclosed. The removal creates a strong in-text contradiction because Section 2 now prohibits actions that Section 4 permits.

perturbation.original_text: 4.The parties agree that information is not to be regarded as Confidential Information and that the Company will have no obligation with respect to any information which the Company can demonstrate: 4.1is publicly available at the time of disclosure; 4.2is or becomes known to the public through no wrongful act of the Company; 4.3is used or disclosed with the prior written authorisation of the Client; or 4.4is disclosed by the Company in compliance with a legal requirement of a government agency or otherwise where disclosure is required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.The parties agree that information is not to be regarded as Confidential Information and that the Company will have no obligation with respect to any information which the Company can demonstrate: 4.1is publicly available at the time of disclosure; 4.2is or becomes known to the public through no wrongful act of the Company; 4.3is used or disclosed with the prior written authorisation of the Client.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or 4.4is disclosed by the Company in compliance with a legal requirement of a government agency or otherwise where disclosure is required by operation of law.' it creates a potential in-text contradiction within the broader legal context. Suppose another clause or a legal jurisdiction obligates the company to disclose specific confidential information to a government agency. Removing section 4.4 creates conflict because the agreement now seems to prohibit any disclosure, even when legally mandated, leading to uncertainty about whether the company must violate the NDA to comply with the law or vice versa.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with English law, and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the company had no obligation to keep information confidential if it was disclosed to comply with a legal requirement. The changed text removes this exception. The contradicted text specifies that the agreement is governed by English law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts. Removing the exception regarding legal requirements in clause 4 creates a potential contradiction because the agreement now appears to prohibit any disclosure, even when legally mandated by English law, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty. Therefore, it's a strong in-text contradiction, adhering to the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition, because it removes language that creates contradiction with obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2The Company acknowledges that damages may not adequately compensate for a breach of this Agreement, and that the Client may also seek such other remedies, including injunctions, as it desires.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2The Company acknowledges that damages may not adequately compensate for a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and that the Client may also seek such other remedies, including injunctions, as it desires.' it limits the client's options in case of a breach. The client is no longer explicitly granted the right to pursue remedies like injunctions. Since section 6.3 states the agreement is governed by English Law, and the legal system inherently allows for various remedies for contract breaches, removing this portion contradicts the general understanding that the client would retain rights granted by applicable law. This creates an in-text contradiction between the limited remedy mentioned and the broader legal framework.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with English law, and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the English Courts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.2 removes the client's explicit right to seek remedies such as injunctions. Section 6.3 states that the agreement is governed by English law. By removing the client's explicit right to seek other remedies, including injunctions, it creates a contradiction because English law inherently allows for various remedies for contract breaches. The changed text limits the remedy, conflicting with the broader legal framework established by section 6.3, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
